cases.title: Shared inference capacity under volatile demand

public.objective: Increase accelerator utilization without turning the platform into a fragile request-path dependency.
facts: Six product teams run latency-sensitive models with sharply different traffic patterns.
Reserved replicas keep 38 percent of accelerator memory idle during most hours.
Two services need sub-second cold-start behavior while the others tolerate up to 45 seconds.
The platform can control placement, quota, admission, and model-cache policy, but service runtimes currently own request routing.
A central proxy would improve global visibility but would add latency and a shared failure domain.
public.decision_request: Choose the next-quarter architecture and rollout sequence for reclaiming idle capacity.
plausible_paths: Build a central request proxy that owns wake-up and routing.
Keep routing inside services and add a platform-owned reclaim and placement control plane.
Buy a managed serving layer and migrate teams behind its runtime contract.
material_constraints: Sub-second cold starts are required for two services.
The platform team has four engineers for the quarter.
A request-path outage would affect all six teams.
Teams use two incompatible serving runtimes.
Quota and cost attribution must remain enforceable.
harmful_failure_paths: A shared proxy becomes a latency and availability bottleneck before operational ownership is mature.
A policy-only approach leaves teams able to reserve memory without enforceable admission or reclaim.
incomplete_information: The managed vendor has not supplied production cold-start data for the larger models.
public.reversible_action: Pilot platform-controlled reclaim with two tolerant services and a tested opt-out before changing request routing.

must_cover_constraints: Separate request routing from capacity governance.
Keep quota and cost attribution enforceable.
acceptable_decision_families: A control-plane reclaim pilot that leaves request routing with runtimes.
A narrowly scoped managed-serving trial for tolerant services with explicit exit criteria.
fatal_errors: Mandate a central proxy for every production request before latency and failover evidence exists.
Leave capacity ownership entirely voluntary while claiming utilization is governed.
known_valid_insights: Cold-start classes can be treated as placement tiers rather than forcing one architecture for all services.
decision_closure_requirements: Select pilot services and an owner.
Define utilization and latency success metrics.
State evidence required before expanding routing ownership.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Both a control-plane pilot and a constrained managed trial can be defensible; the case tests whether the answer separates governance from request routing.

cases.title: Event consistency boundary during schema evolution

public.objective: Reduce consumer breakage without making every producer change wait for central approval.
facts: Forty teams publish events into a shared stream and twelve fields are governed as critical contracts.
Recent incidents came from renamed critical fields and changed unit semantics.
Most weekly changes are additive fields ignored by existing consumers.
The registry can block, warn, quarantine, or shadow-evaluate changes.
Some batch consumers update only once per week and cannot immediately adopt new versions.
public.decision_request: Choose a compatibility policy and migration mechanism for the next two quarters.
plausible_paths: Block every unrecognized schema difference until a central reviewer approves it.
Allow all producer changes and rely on consumer alerts and rollback.
Apply risk-tiered contracts with versioning, quarantine, and monitored additive changes.
material_constraints: Critical owned fields cannot silently change meaning.
Compatible additive fields are common and time-sensitive.
Slow batch consumers need a migration window.
Central review capacity is limited to ten changes per week.
Rollback must preserve replayability.
harmful_failure_paths: Global strictness turns harmless additions into an availability problem.
Permissive acceptance lets semantic breaking changes corrupt downstream decisions.
incomplete_information: The percentage of consumers that validate units rather than field names is unknown.
public.reversible_action: Shadow-classify schema changes for four weeks while enforcing only existing critical contracts.

must_cover_constraints: Differentiate critical semantic changes from additive changes.
Provide migration support for slow consumers.
acceptable_decision_families: Risk-tiered enforcement with versioned critical contracts.
A shadow period followed by selective enforcement based on observed classifications.
fatal_errors: Hard-fail every additive field globally with no compatibility exception.
Accept critical semantic changes until a consumer reports corrupted output.
known_valid_insights: Unit semantics require a contract mechanism beyond structural schema compatibility.
decision_closure_requirements: Specify shadow metrics and stop conditions.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: There is no single required implementation, but a good answer must avoid both blanket blocking and semantic permissiveness.

cases.title: Support copilot rollout with uneven policy risk

public.objective: Reduce handle time while preventing incorrect policy commitments to customers.
facts: An internal copilot cuts median handle time by 18 percent in a 60-agent trial.
Answers about troubleshooting are usually correct, but refund and cancellation answers failed six of 120 reviewed conversations.
Citation display exists for help-center articles but not for account-specific policy exceptions.
Agents can edit suggestions before sending, although fast responders accept suggestions more often.
A customer-visible auto-answer mode could launch in six weeks if approved now.
public.decision_request: Choose the product scope, safeguards, and evidence required for the next release.
plausible_paths: Launch customer-visible auto-answer for all intents.
Keep a human-in-the-loop copilot for every intent.
Segment intents and automation levels based on consequence and evidence quality.
material_constraints: Refund mistakes create financial and trust harm.
Troubleshooting value is already measurable.
Account exceptions lack reliable citations.
Agent acceptance behavior varies.
The team can implement intent routing but not a new policy engine this quarter.
harmful_failure_paths: A broad launch converts known policy uncertainty into direct customer commitments.
A total pause discards a verified low-risk productivity gain.
incomplete_information: The six policy failures may be concentrated in a small number of intent subtypes.
public.reversible_action: Run a routed pilot that auto-suggests low-risk troubleshooting while forcing review for financial-policy intents.

must_cover_constraints: Keep a rapid kill or disable path.
Do not discard the demonstrated internal value.
acceptable_decision_families: A risk-tiered human-in-the-loop rollout.
A narrower troubleshooting-only automation pilot with policy intents excluded.
fatal_errors: Approve unreviewed financial-policy answers despite the observed failure set.
Cancel all internal assistance without considering intent segmentation.
known_valid_insights: Agent acceptance rate by intent is a leading risk metric, not just model accuracy.
decision_closure_requirements: Name the launch owner and review date.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Multiple scoped launches are valid; quality depends on converting heterogeneous risk into an operational decision.

cases.title: Enterprise workflow commitment before discovery

public.objective: Capture a major contract without creating an unbounded bespoke roadmap commitment.
facts: A prospect representing 14 percent of next year's target revenue wants a workflow promised by quarter end.
The requested workflow overlaps the product vision but includes three prospect-specific approval steps.
Engineering estimates range from six to sixteen weeks because data access and audit requirements are unclear.
Two existing customers have expressed adjacent needs but not the exact workflow.
The prospect will fund a paid design phase but wants a delivery date in the master agreement.
public.decision_request: Choose the commercial commitment, discovery scope, and roadmap decision process.
plausible_paths: Promise the full workflow by quarter end and reallocate capacity.
Reject the request to protect the standard roadmap.
Offer a paid design partnership with staged commitments and explicit product-fit gates.
material_constraints: Revenue concentration is material.
Scope and audit requirements are unresolved.
Bespoke work could consume core roadmap capacity.
Adjacent market demand may make part of the request strategic.
Contract language needs a concrete value exchange.
harmful_failure_paths: A fixed delivery promise turns discovery uncertainty into contractual breach risk.
A categorical rejection prevents learning whether a reusable product opportunity exists.
incomplete_information: The reusable portion of the workflow cannot be known before design and data-access discovery.
public.reversible_action: Contract for a time-boxed paid discovery with a prototype and a separately approved delivery option.

must_cover_constraints: Do not convert unresolved scope into a fixed delivery promise.
Preserve a path to learn about reusable demand.
Give the prospect a concrete paid outcome.
Set an explicit decision owner after discovery.
acceptable_decision_families: Paid discovery with no unconditional delivery date.
A narrowly defined reusable milestone with prospect-specific work explicitly excluded.
fatal_errors: Commit the undefined full workflow to a fixed quarter-end date.
Refuse any discovery or design-partner path despite funded learning.
known_valid_insights: An option contract for delivery can create commercial value without pretending discovery is complete.
decision_closure_requirements: State what is and is not contracted.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: The best answer may be a staged commercial structure, but more than one allocation of reusable versus bespoke scope is defensible.

cases.title: Partial degradation with uncertain shared cause

public.objective: Restore reliable service quickly without amplifying an uncertain failure.
facts: Checkout latency is elevated in one region for 18 percent of requests.
A database connection-pool change and a network policy rollout occurred within the same hour.
Rolling back either change takes fifteen minutes; rolling back both loses diagnostic separation.
Traffic can be shifted, but the receiving region is already at 72 percent capacity.
Error rates remain below the contractual threshold while abandonment is rising.
public.decision_request: Choose the immediate mitigation sequence, communication, and diagnostic plan.
plausible_paths: Roll back both recent changes immediately.
Shift all affected traffic and diagnose without rollback.
Apply a staged mitigation that preserves causal information while limiting customer impact.
material_constraints: Customer abandonment is already increasing.
Receiving-region headroom is finite.
Causal evidence will be lost if both changes revert together.
Rollback itself is fast but not risk-free.
External status communication must not claim an unproven cause.
harmful_failure_paths: Waiting for perfect diagnosis prolongs measurable customer harm.
Simultaneous broad changes make the system stable but leave the causal risk ready to recur.
incomplete_information: Trace sampling is too sparse to distinguish pool waits from network retries.
public.reversible_action: Shift a capped traffic slice while reverting one change and increasing trace sampling.

must_cover_constraints: Avoid asserting an unverified root cause.
acceptable_decision_families: Capped traffic shift plus a single-change rollback and observation window.
Single-change rollback first with predeclared traffic-shift trigger.
fatal_errors: Shift all traffic into a region without reserving safety headroom.
Delay every mitigation until the root cause is certain.
known_valid_insights: A sequential intervention can be both mitigation and an information-producing experiment.
decision_closure_requirements: Define the next rollback or failover action.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: The case rewards sequencing and learning under pressure rather than a predetermined rollback choice.

cases.title: Migration canary with asymmetric rollback evidence

public.objective: Begin a high-value data migration without creating an unrecoverable reconciliation problem.
facts: The new ledger pipeline passed three full-volume shadow runs with aggregate totals matching.
Record-level reconciliation still shows 0.08 percent mismatches concentrated in legacy adjustments.
The old pipeline can run in parallel for six weeks but dual operation costs four engineer-days per week.
Rollback restores writes to the old pipeline but does not automatically reverse downstream notifications.
A five-percent customer canary can exclude accounts with legacy adjustments.
public.decision_request: Choose whether and how to start production migration, including reconciliation and rollback controls.
plausible_paths: Launch all customers because aggregate totals reconcile.
Block migration until every record-level mismatch is eliminated.
Run a bounded canary that isolates the unresolved adjustment class.
material_constraints: Downstream notifications complicate rollback.
Mismatch concentration is known but not fully explained.
Dual operation is costly but time-limited.
A clean customer cohort is available.
Finance requires daily signed reconciliation.
harmful_failure_paths: Aggregate-only validation hides customer-level accounting defects.
Demanding zero unexplained differences prevents learning from a safely isolated cohort.
incomplete_information: It is unknown whether the legacy mismatches share one deterministic transformation bug.
public.reversible_action: Canary only non-legacy-adjustment accounts with notification suppression and daily record-level reconciliation.

acceptable_decision_families: A clean-cohort canary with notification controls.
A shadow extension targeted at the mismatch class before a canary.
fatal_errors: Launch every account based only on aggregate reconciliation.
Start a canary with no way to contain downstream notifications.
known_valid_insights: Cohort selection can create rollback safety without requiring the legacy defect to be solved first.
decision_closure_requirements: Set mismatch and notification stop thresholds.
Set a review date for legacy adjustments.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: A pause or canary may both be defensible depending on the proposed controls; blanket launch is not.

cases.title: Diagnostic retention for rare production defects

public.objective: Preserve enough diagnostic evidence for rare defects while reducing sensitive-data exposure.
facts: Raw session traces helped diagnose three severe defects that occurred less than once per million sessions.
The traces contain user text, device identifiers, and occasionally access tokens in malformed headers.
Current retention is 180 days with access available to 42 engineers.
Redaction removes known fields but misses novel malformed payloads.
Sanitized structural traces preserve timing and service topology but not exact user input.
public.decision_request: Choose the retention tiers, access controls, and rare-defect investigation process.
plausible_paths: Retain all raw traces for 180 days to maximize diagnosability.
Delete raw traces immediately after online processing.
Create tiered retention with short raw access and longer sanitized samples.
material_constraints: Rare defects require retrospective evidence.
Tokens in malformed headers create account risk.
Known-field redaction is incomplete.
Forty-two-person raw access is broader than necessary.
Legal hold and incident response may require exceptions.
harmful_failure_paths: Long broad retention converts a debugging asset into a large breach surface.
Immediate deletion makes rare high-impact failures practically uninvestigable.
incomplete_information: The fraction of rare diagnoses that truly required user text rather than structural data is unknown.
public.reversible_action: Run a 30-day tiered-retention pilot with sampled raw escrow and measure diagnostic sufficiency.

must_cover_constraints: Reduce raw-data lifetime and access breadth.
Preserve a path for rare-defect investigation.
Define exceptional legal or incident access.
Measure whether sanitized traces are sufficient.
acceptable_decision_families: Short raw escrow plus longer sanitized sampling.
Default rapid deletion with narrowly triggered, approved capture windows.
fatal_errors: Keep broad raw access for six months without additional controls.
Delete every diagnostic artifact before measuring rare-defect impact.
known_valid_insights: Triggered capture windows can shift raw retention from universal to incident-specific.
decision_closure_requirements: Name access approval and audit rules.
Provide emergency capture and deletion paths.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Two different minimization architectures are acceptable if they retain a controlled rare-defect path.

cases.title: Partner production access before auth integration

public.objective: Enable a time-sensitive partner launch without creating unmanaged privileged access.
facts: The partner needs to invoke two production operations for a launch in ten days.
Its identity platform cannot support the preferred workload federation for another six weeks.
The internal gateway can issue scoped tokens with a 24-hour lifetime and request-level logging.
Manual issuance currently requires a security engineer and has no self-service renewal.
The partner forecasts 600 calls per day and needs continuous availability.
public.decision_request: Choose an interim access design, operating model, and launch condition.
plausible_paths: Issue one long-lived integration administrator credential.
Delay launch until workload federation is complete.
Operate a short-lived scoped-token bridge with explicit renewal and monitoring.
material_constraints: Only two operations are required.
Continuous operation makes purely ad hoc issuance fragile.
Security staffing cannot renew tokens manually every hour.
Request-level logging is available.
The preferred identity path has a credible six-week date.
harmful_failure_paths: A broad persistent credential outlives the launch pressure and obscures accountability.
A nominally secure bridge fails operationally and causes unsafe emergency workarounds.
incomplete_information: The partner's deployment platform support for automated 24-hour renewal is untested.
public.reversible_action: Test automated renewal in staging and issue production scope only after a monitored 72-hour soak.

must_cover_constraints: Keep a six-week exit to federation.
acceptable_decision_families: A monitored short-lived bridge after renewal testing.
A brief launch delay if automated renewal cannot pass the soak.
fatal_errors: Issue a shared long-lived administrator secret for convenience.
Approve production access without a reliable renewal path or named operator.
known_valid_insights: Operational fragility is a security control failure because it creates pressure for manual bypasses.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Launching or delaying can both be correct depending on renewal evidence; a good answer makes that evidence the decision gate.

cases.title: Model-provider dependence ahead of launch

public.objective: Ship a differentiated feature while containing switching, data-handling, and outage risk.
facts: Provider A is the only tested provider meeting current latency and tool-use quality targets.
Provider A's new caching API could cut cost by 30 percent but is proprietary.
A second provider passes simple prompts but has not passed tool-use or safety evaluation.
A full provider-neutral abstraction is estimated at twelve engineer-weeks.
A thin request, logging, policy, and fallback boundary is estimated at three weeks.
public.decision_request: Choose the launch architecture and the amount of portability investment before release.
plausible_paths: Use Provider A APIs directly and defer portability.
Delay launch for a complete provider-neutral platform.
Launch on A behind a thin volatile-boundary seam and test a limited fallback.
material_constraints: The feature date matters commercially.
Only A currently meets quality targets.
Data-handling flags differ by request.
Fallback quality is unproven.
Proprietary caching creates savings and coupling.
harmful_failure_paths: Deep direct integration spreads provider semantics across product workflows.
A universal abstraction erases provider-specific quality controls and delays all learning.
incomplete_information: The revenue sensitivity to a three-week delay is uncertain.
public.reversible_action: Launch an internal slice through the thin seam and run shadow evaluation against Provider B.

must_cover_constraints: Do not claim an untested fallback is production-ready.
acceptable_decision_families: Launch on A behind a thin seam with shadow fallback testing.
A short delay to finish only the thin seam and critical safety evaluation.
fatal_errors: Spread proprietary provider calls throughout product code with no boundary.
Block launch until every provider behaves through a universal abstraction.
known_valid_insights: Portability should target volatile policy and observability surfaces, not lowest-common-denominator model behavior.
decision_closure_requirements: Choose the launch provider and boundary.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: The case admits a short delay or immediate bounded launch depending on sequencing; it rejects both hard-coded sprawl and abstraction maximalism.

cases.title: Accelerator reservation versus cloud burst capacity

public.objective: Secure enough accelerator capacity for growth without locking the company into an inflexible demand forecast.
facts: Base inference demand is expected to grow between 35 and 90 percent next year.
A three-year reserved cluster costs 42 percent less per accelerator-hour at 75 percent utilization.
Cloud burst capacity is available but has shown two regional shortages during major launches.
Model efficiency work could cut required compute by 25 percent but has not shipped.
The reservation can be expanded after nine months but cannot be reduced.
public.decision_request: Choose the reservation, burst, and efficiency-investment portfolio.
plausible_paths: Reserve capacity for the high-growth forecast.
Stay fully on demand until the forecast stabilizes.
Reserve a base tranche and contract burst plus efficiency milestones.
material_constraints: Launch shortages have revenue impact.
Demand uncertainty is wide.
Reservation downside is asymmetric.
Efficiency work competes with product engineering.
Regional concentration affects resilience.
harmful_failure_paths: A high-case reservation converts forecast optimism into years of stranded capital.
A fully variable plan treats historical shortage risk as costless.
incomplete_information: The provider has not committed a price for guaranteed burst capacity.
public.reversible_action: Negotiate a base reservation with a nine-month expansion option and a priced burst capacity test.

must_cover_constraints: Quantify downside of under- and over-reservation.
Treat efficiency as uncertain capacity supply.
acceptable_decision_families: Base reservation plus contracted burst and efficiency gates.
Smaller reservation plus a second-region or second-vendor resilience purchase.
fatal_errors: Reserve the full high-growth forecast without downside controls.
Rely only on unguaranteed on-demand capacity despite prior launch shortages.
known_valid_insights: Efficiency milestones can be valued as a real option but should not be counted as delivered capacity.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Several portfolio weights are valid; quality comes from making uncertainty and options explicit.

cases.title: Federated on-call ownership for a shared platform

public.objective: Improve incident response for a shared platform without destroying service-team accountability.
facts: The platform has twelve services owned by five teams and a central SRE group of four.
Thirty percent of pages require a service owner, but routing often starts with central SRE.
Two teams lack enough engineers for a sustainable 24/7 rotation.
Central SRE can diagnose infrastructure quickly but lacks application context.
Recent incidents had duplicate responders and no single incident owner.
public.decision_request: Choose the on-call topology, escalation contract, and transition plan.
plausible_paths: Centralize all first-line and resolution ownership in SRE.
Require every service team to own a complete rotation.
Use a shared first-response layer with explicit service ownership and capability tiers.
material_constraints: Small teams cannot staff full rotations safely.
Application context is distributed.
Infrastructure diagnosis benefits from central expertise.
Every incident needs one commander.
Paging load and training must remain sustainable.
harmful_failure_paths: Full centralization turns SRE into an unbounded application support queue.
Uniform federation creates unsafe rotations for teams below staffing minimums.
incomplete_information: Page volume by service is incomplete because several alerts share generic routing.
public.reversible_action: Pilot a shared first-response rotation for three services with explicit transfer and incident-command rules.

must_cover_constraints: Protect small teams from unsafe rotations.
acceptable_decision_families: Tiered shared first response with named service escalation.
Service ownership with central SRE coverage only for teams below a staffing threshold.
fatal_errors: Make central SRE permanently responsible for every application incident.
Require understaffed teams to operate independent round-the-clock rotations immediately.
known_valid_insights: Capability tiers can make ownership conditional without making it ambiguous.
decision_closure_requirements: Define first response and resolution ownership.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: The answer need not choose one universal topology; it must produce a clear operating contract.

cases.title: Incentive metric that drives local optimization

public.objective: Improve response efficiency without rewarding behavior that increases repeat contacts or customer harm.
facts: Teams are currently rewarded for median handle time and tickets closed per day.
Handle time improved 22 percent while seven-day repeat contacts rose 11 percent.
Complex cases are concentrated among three specialist groups.
Customer satisfaction is sampled for only eight percent of cases.
A new case-complexity classifier is 82 percent accurate and uneven across languages.
public.decision_request: Choose next-quarter incentives, measurement, and transition safeguards.
plausible_paths: Keep productivity targets and add a customer-satisfaction bonus.
Replace throughput metrics with quality and repeat-contact metrics.
Use a balanced scorecard with complexity adjustment and guarded rollout.
material_constraints: Repeat contacts reflect hidden cost and customer friction.
Quality sampling is sparse.
Complexity differs materially by team.
The classifier has language bias.
Abrupt target changes affect staffing and compensation.
harmful_failure_paths: Adding a small quality weight leaves throughput gaming economically dominant.
Removing every productivity signal makes staffing and backlog management blind.
incomplete_information: The causal share of repeat contacts attributable to faster handling is estimated, not proven.
public.reversible_action: Shadow-score the balanced metric for one month and run a capped incentive pilot in two comparable teams.

must_cover_constraints: Adjust for complexity without trusting a biased classifier blindly.
acceptable_decision_families: A shadowed balanced scorecard with capped incentive impact.
A quality-gated productivity metric with manual complexity review during calibration.
fatal_errors: Continue rewarding closure volume while ignoring the repeat-contact increase.
Base compensation directly on the uncorrected language-biased classifier.
known_valid_insights: Repeat-contact cost can serve as a broader quality proxy while satisfaction sampling improves.
decision_closure_requirements: Define the proposed metric weights or gates.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: A weighted score or gated metric can both work if gaming and measurement bias are controlled.

cases.title: Multi-region routing under inconsistent failover state

public.objective: Improve regional resilience without routing writes through stale authorization or inventory state.
facts: Read traffic can fail over globally in under one minute.
Write authorization state replicates in three to twelve minutes during peak load.
Two products tolerate queued writes while one requires immediate confirmation.
Global active-active writes would require conflict handling not yet implemented.
The last regional outage lasted 28 minutes and caused manual routing changes.
public.decision_request: Choose the failover policy, product tiers, and implementation sequence.
plausible_paths: Enable global active-active routing for every request.
Keep all writes regional and accept outage unavailability.
Tier failover by read/write semantics and product tolerance.
material_constraints: Authorization state can be stale.
Products have different confirmation requirements.
Conflict resolution is absent.
Manual failover is slow.
Queued writes need customer-visible semantics.
harmful_failure_paths: Immediate cross-region writes violate authorization or inventory correctness.
Uniform regional pinning leaves tolerant products unnecessarily unavailable.
incomplete_information: Peak replication lag under a prolonged outage has not been load-tested.
public.reversible_action: Pilot read failover and queued-write mode for one tolerant product with forced lag injection.

must_cover_constraints: Separate reads, queued writes, and immediate writes.
acceptable_decision_families: Tiered automated failover with queued writes for tolerant products.
Read-only automation first while write replication and conflict evidence mature.
fatal_errors: Route every write globally before conflict and authorization safety exist.
Keep every product unavailable during regional loss despite safe read or queue paths.
known_valid_insights: Failover capability is a product-specific contract, not one platform boolean.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Multiple sequencing choices are valid, but correctness semantics must lead routing policy.

cases.title: Batch and interactive accelerator scheduling

public.objective: Increase batch throughput while protecting interactive latency and avoiding permanent overprovisioning.
facts: Interactive inference uses 45 to 80 percent of the cluster depending on hour.
Batch evaluation jobs can checkpoint every twenty minutes but lose work when preempted.
Product teams reserve headroom based on launch forecasts that are often early.
A queue priority system exists but has no price or quota signal.
A separate batch cluster would take eight weeks to procure.
public.decision_request: Choose the scheduling policy and capacity investment for the next quarter.
plausible_paths: Preempt batch whenever interactive demand rises.
Build a separate dedicated batch cluster.
Use quota-priced elastic headroom with bounded preemption and checkpoint incentives.
material_constraints: Interactive latency has contractual targets.
Frequent batch preemption wastes compute.
Forecast reservations are noisy.
Procurement lead time is material.
Teams need predictable batch completion windows.
harmful_failure_paths: Unlimited preemption makes nominal spare capacity unusable for important batch work.
Dedicated procurement locks in capacity before scheduling economics are understood.
incomplete_information: The distribution of batch checkpoint recovery time is not instrumented.
public.reversible_action: Pilot bounded preemption with checkpoint telemetry and reserved batch windows.

acceptable_decision_families: Bounded preemption plus reserved windows and quota signals.
A temporary capacity tranche combined with measured scheduling reform.
fatal_errors: Allow batch jobs to be killed without bounds or recovery measurement.
Procure a permanent isolated cluster without testing shared scheduling.
known_valid_insights: Checkpoint cadence can become an economic scheduling input rather than a fixed application choice.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: A small capacity purchase can be valid, but only as part of an explicit option and measurement plan.

cases.title: Personalization ramp with sparse segment evidence

public.objective: Capture aggregate recommendation lift without harming under-measured user segments.
facts: Offline evaluation predicts a 4.5 percent conversion lift overall.
Two small language segments show lower relevance, but each has fewer than 300 labeled examples.
Online logging cannot yet identify one of those segments reliably.
A five-percent ramp with manual cohort analysis is available within one week.
The current system has stable but lower aggregate conversion.
public.decision_request: Choose the launch ramp, measurement work, and decision thresholds.
plausible_paths: Launch to all users to realize aggregate lift.
Reject the model until every segment improves offline.
Run a guarded ramp with improved segment observability and explicit tradeoff ownership.
material_constraints: Aggregate benefit is plausible.
Segment harm evidence is noisy but directional.
One segment cannot be monitored live.
A limited ramp is operationally possible.
Leadership must own any accepted segment tradeoff.
harmful_failure_paths: Full rollout hides harm behind an aggregate metric.
Permanent rejection treats sparse uncertainty as proof that no safe learning path exists.
incomplete_information: The true online conversion and retention effect for the two segments is unknown.
public.reversible_action: Instrument segment proxies and run a capped ramp with daily manual review.

must_cover_constraints: Improve segment observability before broad exposure.
acceptable_decision_families: A small guarded ramp after minimum segment logging.
A short instrumentation delay followed by a predefined ramp.
fatal_errors: Approve full rollout while one at-risk segment is not observable.
Discard the model permanently based only on sparse offline labels.
known_valid_insights: The first decision is about observability sufficiency, not the final universal rollout.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Either a brief delay or a very small ramp is defensible if the observability boundary is explicit.

cases.title: Pricing experiment with renewal and fairness exposure

public.objective: Learn willingness to pay without creating opaque or unfair treatment for renewing customers.
facts: A proposed price package increases projected new-customer revenue by 12 percent.
The experiment tool can randomize new signups but not reliably exclude accounts linked to existing organizations.
Renewing customers have contractual notice periods.
Sales can manually override quotes but override reasons are not logged.
The team can run a geography-limited test or wait six weeks for account-linking fixes.
public.decision_request: Choose experiment scope, customer protections, and learning criteria.
plausible_paths: Run the global experiment immediately.
Wait for perfect identity linking before any test.
Run a bounded cohort test with notice, override logging, and exclusion controls.
material_constraints: Renewal contracts constrain price changes.
Organization-linked accounts may cross cohorts.
Manual overrides can bias results.
Pricing learning has commercial value.
A geography-limited cohort reduces sample speed.
harmful_failure_paths: Existing customers receive inconsistent treatment without notice.
Waiting for perfect infrastructure delays every low-risk learning path.
incomplete_information: The percentage of signups linked to existing organizations is estimated at three to ten percent.
public.reversible_action: Run a small geography cohort with manual organization screening and logged overrides.

acceptable_decision_families: A geography-limited protected cohort.
A short delay for minimum linking controls followed by a broader test.
fatal_errors: Randomize potentially renewing accounts without notice or exclusion.
Treat unlogged sales overrides as harmless to experimental validity.
known_valid_insights: Override logging is both a fairness control and a source of causal-confounding evidence.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: The timing can vary; a good answer integrates customer contract and experimental validity.

cases.title: Incident communication before root cause certainty

public.objective: Give customers useful, accurate guidance while engineers investigate an intermittent data-delay event.
facts: Some dashboards are delayed by five to forty minutes across two regions.
Data remains durable and no corruption is observed.
A queue backlog is visible, but the initiating cause may be an upstream retry storm or a storage throttle.
Enterprise customers are making operational decisions from the dashboards.
A temporary freshness indicator can ship in ninety minutes.
public.decision_request: Choose the external message, product mitigation, and update cadence.
plausible_paths: Wait to communicate until the initiating cause is known.
Declare a storage-provider failure and promise a recovery time.
Communicate verified impact and uncertainty while adding freshness visibility.
material_constraints: Customers need to know data may be stale.
Durability appears intact.
Root cause is not confirmed.
A product-level freshness mitigation is available.
Recovery timing is variable.
harmful_failure_paths: Silence lets customers treat stale data as current.
A confident unverified cause or ETA damages trust when evidence changes.
incomplete_information: The backlog drain rate changes as retries are throttled.
public.reversible_action: Publish impact and uncertainty now, add the freshness indicator, and update on a fixed cadence.

acceptable_decision_families: Immediate uncertainty-aware communication plus freshness UI.
Immediate targeted enterprise outreach followed by public status once the indicator ships.
fatal_errors: Withhold all communication while customers rely on stale dashboards.
Blame a specific provider and promise an unsupported recovery time.
known_valid_insights: Product observability can reduce customer harm before the infrastructure root cause is resolved.
decision_closure_requirements: State what evidence will close the incident.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: The precise communication channel is flexible; evidence discipline and customer actionability are central.

cases.title: Maintenance deferral before seasonal peak

public.objective: Manage a known component failure risk without creating avoidable peak-period outage exposure.
facts: A database storage component has a vendor advisory for a failure mode observed in 0.4 percent of similar fleets.
Replacing it requires a two-hour maintenance window and a tested failover.
Seasonal traffic peak begins in twelve days and lasts five weeks.
The most recent failover test passed, but application reconnect time exceeded the target by 40 seconds.
The vendor can provide an on-site spare in four days.
public.decision_request: Choose whether to maintain before peak, defer, or apply an alternate risk treatment.
plausible_paths: Replace immediately using the tested failover.
Defer until after peak and monitor.
Stage the spare, improve reconnect behavior, and use a conditional pre-peak window.
material_constraints: The component risk is low-probability but high-impact.
Peak outage cost is elevated.
Failover reconnect misses target.
A spare can arrive before peak.
A maintenance window affects customers.
harmful_failure_paths: Rushing maintenance treats a degraded failover test as fully safe.
Deferring without contingency leaves a known failure mode exposed through peak.
incomplete_information: Vendor telemetry cannot predict whether this specific component is in the affected population.
public.reversible_action: Stage the spare and rerun failover after a reconnect fix before selecting the final pre-peak window.

acceptable_decision_families: Conditional pre-peak maintenance after reconnect retest.
Peak deferral with staged spare, enhanced monitoring, and practiced emergency replacement.
fatal_errors: Proceed immediately while ignoring the failed reconnect objective.
Defer through peak with no staged spare or emergency plan.
known_valid_insights: The spare and reconnect retest create an option that improves both maintenance and deferral choices.
decision_closure_requirements: Name the maintenance or deferral owner.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Either final timing can be defensible; the answer must buy information and create contingency before the deadline.

cases.title: Privacy-preserving product analytics

public.objective: Measure feature adoption and retention while reducing the identifiability of behavioral data.
facts: Product teams currently join event-level behavior to customer accounts for cohort analysis.
Most roadmap decisions need weekly segment trends, but a fraud team needs event-level sequences for investigations.
A proposed aggregation pipeline can provide weekly cohorts with a minimum group size of fifty.
Some enterprise segments have fewer than fifty active users.
Raw event access is available to 27 analysts with inconsistent query logging.
public.decision_request: Choose the analytics architecture, exception process, and migration sequence.
plausible_paths: Keep event-level account joins for every analyst.
Move all analysis to aggregate cohorts with no raw exceptions.
Default to aggregates while creating narrow audited investigative access.
material_constraints: Most decisions do not require identity-level data.
Fraud investigations need sequences.
Small enterprise cohorts can disappear under minimum-group rules.
Current access logging is inconsistent.
Migration cannot break committed customer reporting.
harmful_failure_paths: Broad raw access remains the default because a narrow use case exists.
Universal aggregation makes legitimate investigation and small-cohort reporting impossible.
incomplete_information: The fraud team's actual monthly use of identity-level sequences is not measured.
public.reversible_action: Move two product teams to aggregate defaults while logging and reviewing all raw fraud queries.

acceptable_decision_families: Aggregate-by-default with narrow approved raw access.
Pseudonymous event sequences with re-identification only under investigation approval.
fatal_errors: Leave unrestricted account-linked analytics as the universal default.
Remove every event-sequence path needed for active fraud investigations.
known_valid_insights: Pseudonymous sequences can preserve temporal fraud signals while separating routine analysts from identity.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Several privacy architectures are acceptable if use-case separation is operational rather than rhetorical.

cases.title: Emergency administration during identity outage

public.objective: Restore critical operations during an identity-provider outage without normalizing untracked break-glass access.
facts: The primary identity provider has been unavailable for 35 minutes.
Production services remain healthy, but operators cannot approve deployments or rotate a failing certificate.
Two offline break-glass accounts exist; one was tested last month and both require hardware keys.
The certificate expires in three hours.
Audit ingestion is delayed but local signed logs remain available.
public.decision_request: Choose whether and how to activate emergency access, including scope and recovery.
plausible_paths: Wait for identity recovery to avoid emergency credentials.
Share a break-glass account across the operations team.
Activate a named, scoped break-glass session with dual control and local audit.
material_constraints: Certificate rotation has a three-hour deadline.
One emergency account has recent test evidence.
Hardware keys limit who can act.
Central audit is delayed.
Emergency access must be revoked and reviewed.
harmful_failure_paths: Waiting converts an identity outage into a service outage.
Shared emergency access destroys attribution and may persist after recovery.
incomplete_information: Identity-provider recovery time is unknown.
public.reversible_action: Activate the tested account for a named operator and approver, rotate only the certificate, then revoke.

acceptable_decision_families: Immediate scoped activation of the tested account.
A short timed wait with automatic scoped activation well before the certificate deadline.
fatal_errors: Distribute one emergency credential to the whole operations team.
Refuse every emergency path until the certificate expires.
known_valid_insights: A delayed central audit can remain acceptable if local logs are signed, preserved, and reconciled.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: The activation timing may vary, but the control and deadline logic should be explicit.

cases.title: Build versus buy for enterprise search

public.objective: Deliver enterprise search relevance quickly while preserving control over permissions and domain quality.
facts: A vendor can launch in four months and leads internal prototypes on general document relevance.
The vendor's permission model does not support two customer-specific inheritance rules.
An internal system could support permissions but is estimated at nine to fifteen months.
Search quality depends on domain-specific feedback that customers consider sensitive.
The vendor offers data-isolation terms but charges heavily for custom connectors.
public.decision_request: Choose build, buy, or staged sourcing and define the decision gates.
plausible_paths: Buy the vendor product and adapt customer permissions.
Build the full system internally.
Buy or partner for commodity retrieval while retaining permission and feedback boundaries.
material_constraints: Time to market matters.
Permission correctness is non-negotiable.
Domain feedback is strategically valuable.
Internal estimates are wide.
Connector economics affect margins.
harmful_failure_paths: A fast vendor launch silently weakens customer authorization semantics.
A full internal build delays learning and rebuilds commodity capabilities.
incomplete_information: The vendor has not completed a proof of concept for the two inheritance rules.
public.reversible_action: Fund an eight-week proof of concept with a thin permission boundary and representative connectors.

must_cover_constraints: Define an exit from vendor dependence.
acceptable_decision_families: A vendor proof with internally owned permission and feedback layers.
An internal permission core combined with purchased retrieval components.
fatal_errors: Launch despite known permission-rule gaps.
Approve a full internal rebuild before testing commodity vendor components.
known_valid_insights: Permission correctness and relevance retrieval can be sourced through different boundaries.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: The exact sourcing split is open; a good answer decomposes strategic and commodity layers.

cases.title: Sole-supplier component before committed launch

public.objective: Protect a committed launch date without accepting an unmanaged single-supplier failure.
facts: A specialized component has one qualified supplier with 96 percent historical on-time delivery.
The next shipment is required in eight weeks and the supplier reports a possible two-week slip.
A second supplier can produce samples in six weeks but qualification normally takes ten.
Product inventory supports only five days of launch demand.
A reduced-feature configuration avoids the component but lowers expected conversion.
public.decision_request: Choose supply, qualification, and launch-contingency actions now.
plausible_paths: Trust the incumbent schedule and keep the planned configuration.
Delay launch until the second supplier is fully qualified.
Parallelize expedited qualification, inventory actions, and a reduced-feature contingency.
material_constraints: Launch demand exceeds inventory.
The slip is possible, not certain.
Qualification protects product safety and quality.
Reduced features affect conversion.
Decisions must be made before sample arrival.
harmful_failure_paths: Optimistic reliance leaves no launch option if the shipment slips.
Immediate delay discards the chance to qualify or use a controlled alternate configuration.
incomplete_information: Expedited qualification may or may not validate the second supplier in time.
public.reversible_action: Start expedited sample qualification and prepare a limited reduced-feature launch decision gate.

acceptable_decision_families: Parallel qualification plus staged configuration contingency.
A deliberately smaller initial launch backed by incumbent and alternate milestones.
fatal_errors: Rely solely on the incumbent delivery hope with no contingency.
Ship unqualified alternate components to preserve the date.
known_valid_insights: Launch size is another controllable variable, not just date or component choice.
decision_closure_requirements: Name the final launch decision owner.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Several staged portfolios are valid; the response must avoid false certainty and unsafe qualification shortcuts.

cases.title: Ownership of a shared developer platform

public.objective: Give a shared deployment platform clear product ownership without separating it from operational reality.
facts: A central platform team owns the control plane, while four service teams maintain custom deployment extensions.
Roadmap requests arrive through tickets with no common prioritization.
Service teams blame the platform for incidents caused by unsupported extensions.
The platform team has usage telemetry but little customer-research capacity.
A proposed platform product manager could join centrally or rotate from service teams.
public.decision_request: Choose ownership, extension governance, and roadmap decision rights.
plausible_paths: Centralize all extensions and roadmap decisions in the platform team.
Let service teams own their own platform behavior and treat the core as infrastructure.
Create a platform product model with explicit core and extension contracts.
material_constraints: Operational incidents cross ownership boundaries.
Extensions contain real domain needs.
Central capacity is limited.
Roadmap demand lacks a shared value model.
Adoption telemetry is available.
harmful_failure_paths: Central ownership absorbs every bespoke requirement and becomes a bottleneck.
Pure federation leaves shared contracts and incident accountability ambiguous.
incomplete_information: The share of extensions that could become reusable core capabilities has not been reviewed.
public.reversible_action: Run a quarterly product council and classify existing extensions against a frozen core contract.

acceptable_decision_families: Central platform product ownership with governed extensions.
A federated product council with a centrally enforced core contract.
fatal_errors: Make the central team responsible for every unsupported extension.
Allow extensions to change shared behavior without contracts or incident ownership.
known_valid_insights: Extension promotion into core can be governed as a product lifecycle rather than an ownership argument.
decision_closure_requirements: Define the pilot review and rollback.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: A centralized or federated council can work if contracts and incentives are explicit.

cases.title: Cross-team roadmap arbitration under shared deadline

public.objective: Allocate scarce engineering capacity across a regulatory deadline, reliability debt, and a revenue opportunity.
facts: A regulatory reporting change is due in fourteen weeks with penalties for noncompliance.
A reliability program would reduce a recurring incident class but has slipped twice.
A major customer will expand if one integration ships in twelve weeks.
The three efforts share four senior data engineers, enough to fully staff only two.
Contractors can add implementation capacity but not replace the senior domain knowledge.
public.decision_request: Choose the portfolio, staffing moves, and evidence-based review points.
plausible_paths: Prioritize regulation and customer expansion, deferring reliability.
Prioritize regulation and reliability, declining the customer date.
Rescope all three with staged deliverables and explicit risk acceptance.
material_constraints: The regulatory date is externally fixed.
Reliability incidents have ongoing customer cost.
The expansion has meaningful revenue.
Senior domain knowledge is the binding resource.
Scope can change, but hidden parallel work cannot create more senior capacity.
harmful_failure_paths: Nominally staffing all three produces three late programs.
Treating either revenue or reliability as automatically subordinate hides a material tradeoff.
incomplete_information: The customer may accept a staged integration, but sales has not tested that option.
public.reversible_action: Negotiate a staged customer milestone while freezing a minimum regulatory team and time-boxing reliability triage.

must_cover_constraints: Use contractors only where domain knowledge is not binding.
acceptable_decision_families: Regulation plus staged customer delivery with a bounded reliability intervention.
Regulation plus reliability, paired with a negotiated customer discovery milestone.
fatal_errors: Declare all three fully staffed without resolving the senior-engineer constraint.
Ignore the fixed compliance deadline while optimizing internal priorities.
known_valid_insights: The customer commitment can be decomposed into value milestones that use less scarce domain capacity.
adjudication.reasoning_notes: Two portfolio choices can be defensible; the answer must expose resource reality and staged options.
